not_applicable_to_note: The mandatory Schedule FA disclosure below applies to a Resident and Ordinarily Resident (ROR). A non-resident or a Resident but Not Ordinarily Resident (RNOR) is generally outside Schedule FA; see nri-residential-status.json for how status is decided.

verification.note: For a Resident and Ordinarily Resident, foreign investments create obligations that a purely domestic portfolio does not. (1) Disclosure: every foreign asset held at ANY point during the calendar year ending 31-Dec-2025 must be reported in Schedule FA of ITR-2 or ITR-3 - foreign stocks, RSUs, ESPP, ETFs, foreign bank/brokerage/custodial accounts, foreign life-insurance with cash value, foreign property, even a dormant account. There is no minimum value threshold for the disclosure itself, and ITR-1/ITR-4 cannot be used. Note Schedule FA uses the calendar year (Jan-Dec), unlike capital gains which use the financial year (Apr-Mar); convert using the SBI TT buying rate. (2) Income: foreign dividends and interest are taxable in India at slab rate under income from other sources; capital gains on foreign shares go in Schedule CG (foreign shares are unlisted-in-India, so 24 months is the long-term threshold at 12.5%); RSU/ESPP vesting is a salary perquisite. (3) Foreign tax credit: tax paid abroad can be credited under Section 90/91 by filing Form 67 (before the return) plus Schedule FSI and Schedule TR. (4) TCS on remittances: from 1-Apr-2025 (Finance Act 2025) the Section 206C(1G) LRS threshold rose from Rs 7 lakh to Rs 10 lakh, TCS is 20% on most investment/gift remittances above Rs 10 lakh, education-loan-funded remittances are fully exempt, and TCS is a prepaid credit recoverable in the ITR, not a cost. (5) Penalties: non-disclosure or inaccurate disclosure of a foreign asset carries a flat Rs 10 lakh penalty per year under Section 43 of the Black Money Act (with a carve-out from 1-Oct-2024 where aggregate undisclosed movable foreign assets are Rs 20 lakh or less - the penalty is waived but the duty to disclose is not), plus 30% tax and up to 3x penalty and prosecution in serious cases. Cross-checked against incometax.gov.in Schedule FA guide, taxguru.in, vested.blog, and Finance Act 2025 summaries; confirm against incometax.gov.in before relying on this as filing advice.

source_refs: Black Money (Undisclosed Foreign Income and Assets) and Imposition of Tax Act, 2015, Sections 41, 42, 43

schedule_fa_disclosure.reporting_period_note: Report assets held at any time during the calendar year ending 31-Dec-2025 (Jan-Dec), not the financial year. Even one day of holding triggers disclosure.
covered_assets: foreign shares, ETFs and mutual funds
RSUs and ESPP from a foreign employer
foreign bank, brokerage and custodial accounts (including dormant)
foreign life insurance or annuity with a cash value
any beneficial ownership, beneficiary interest or signing authority in a foreign account

income_taxation.foreign_dividends_and_interest: Taxable in India at slab rate under income from other sources.
income_taxation.capital_gains_on_foreign_shares: Reported in Schedule CG. Foreign shares are treated like unlisted shares: long-term after 24 months at 12.5%, otherwise short-term at slab rate.
income_taxation.rsu_espp_vesting: Value at vesting is a salary perquisite taxed at slab rate; a later sale is a capital gain.

foreign_tax_credit.note: Tax paid abroad on foreign income can be credited against Indian tax on the same income. Form 67 must be filed on the e-filing portal on or before filing the return.

tcs_on_lrs_remittances.overseas_tour_package: 2% on the whole amount, no threshold
tcs_on_lrs_remittances.note: From 1-Apr-2025 (Finance Act 2025) the exemption threshold rose from Rs 7 lakh to Rs 10 lakh per financial year across all LRS remittances combined. TCS is a prepaid tax credit shown in Form 26AS/AIS and recoverable in the ITR, not an added cost.

small_asset_carve_out.note: From 1-Oct-2024 the Section 43 flat penalty does not apply where aggregate undisclosed movable foreign assets (excluding immovable property) are Rs 20 lakh or less. The penalty is waived; the duty to disclose in Schedule FA is not.

black_money_act_penalties.serious_case: Undisclosed foreign income/asset can be taxed at 30% plus a penalty of up to three times the tax, plus prosecution (Sections 41, 49, 50).

new_act_2025_renumbering.return_filing_provision: Section 263 of the Income Tax Act, 2025
new_act_2025_renumbering.note: The Income Tax Act, 2025 continues the mandatory foreign-asset filing requirement for a resident other than not ordinarily resident. The Black Money Act, 2015 is unchanged. For a FY 2025-26 (AY 2026-27) return the 1961 Act provisions apply.
